pageTitle: Kiln Loading Calculator - Pottery Firing Capacity | GetNiftyKit
metaDescription: Enter your kiln size, shelf (bat) size, and piece size, and this free pottery tool calculates how many pieces fit per shelf, how many shelves you need, and your total firing capacity.
softwareAppDescription: A free pottery tool that calculates total kiln firing capacity from kiln size, shelf (bat) size, and piece size — pieces per shelf multiplied by how many shelf layers fit.
subtitle: Enter your kiln, shelf, and piece sizes to see how many pieces fit in one firing
taglineExtra: Multiplies pieces per shelf by how many layers stack, to tell you the total capacity of your kiln.
invalidInputError: Please enter all values as numbers greater than 0.
shelfTooBigError: The shelf is larger than the kiln interior. Please double-check the shelf size.
noFitError: Not even one piece fits on the shelf at this size. Try reducing the gap or piece size.
kilnTooShortError: The kiln isn't tall enough to fit even one layer. Try reducing the piece height or clearance.
diagramAriaLabel: Diagram of shelf layout and kiln layers
disclaimerText: This is an approximation based on a grid layout — actual capacity varies with piece shape and arrangement. Round pieces are conservatively fit into square cells, so more may actually fit depending on how they're arranged.
infoIntroH2: What is a kiln loading calculator?
infoIntroP: The Kiln Loading Calculator works out how many pieces you can fit into a kiln for one firing before you start loading. First, it calculates how many pieces fit across a single shelf (or "bat") — width and depth divided by piece size after subtracting the gap between pieces and the margin around the shelf's edge. Then it calculates how many shelf layers fit within the kiln's height, based on piece height plus shelf thickness plus the clearance needed above each shelf for even heat circulation. Multiplying these two numbers gives you the total number of pieces that fit in the kiln at once.
infoHowtoP: Enter your kiln's interior size (width, depth, height) and your shelf size (width, depth, thickness). Choose whether your pieces are round, square, or rectangular, then enter the diameter (round), side length (square), or width and depth (rectangular), plus height. The gap between pieces, the shelf edge margin, and the clearance above each shelf all come pre-filled with commonly used defaults — adjust them only if you need to. Once everything's entered, pieces per shelf, shelves needed, and total firing capacity are calculated instantly, along with a diagram.
infoUseCases: Planning how many firings you'll need for an upcoming batch
Estimating loading efficiency before buying a new kiln or shelf set
Comparing different piece sizes or spacing to see which layout is most efficient
Planning firing schedules and production capacity for a studio

faq.q: Is the calculation accurate for round pieces too?
faq.a: Round pieces are conservatively fit into square cells sized to their diameter. In practice, staggered (hexagonal) arrangements can fit slightly more, but this calculator gives you a safe minimum estimate.

faq.q: Why do I need clearance above the shelf?
faq.a: It's standard practice to leave some space between a piece and the shelf above it so heat can circulate evenly during firing. Accounting for this clearance is what makes the layer count accurate.

faq.q: Could more pieces actually fit than the calculated number?
faq.a: Yes. This calculator is an approximation based on a grid layout, so depending on piece shape and how you actually arrange them, more may fit than the calculated value.

privacyNote: This tool performs all calculations entirely in your browser. Nothing you enter is sent to or stored on a server.
